items.memory: When I was 0 years old, raced the other children through the winding tunnels. Thulzol was there too.
items.reflection: A good ale and a warm forge — what more could one want?

items.memory: When I was 1 years old, got lost in the caves for three days — the darkness taught me. Dumdwal was there too.
items.reflection: The deep halls are silent today, and that silence speaks.

items.memory: When I was 2 years old, carved my first stone figurine from a chunk of granite.
items.reflection: Gold is heavy, but the mountain is heavier on the soul.

items.memory: When I was 3 years old, found a sparkling gem in the tailings and kept it forever. The pattern held, as it always does. Dumdwal was there too.
items.reflection: The stone remembers what the surface forgets.

items.memory: When I was 4 years old, was taught the runes of the deep earth by the elder. It was proper — how things should be.
items.reflection: The mountain gives, and the mountain takes.

items.memory: When I was 5 years old, watched the forges burn all night, mesmerized by the glow. Vbazoth was there too.
items.reflection: I can hear the stone breathing around me.

items.memory: When I was 6 years old, helped my father sort ore in the great sorting hall.
items.reflection: Every strike of the pick is a prayer to the deep.

items.memory: When I was 7 years old, drank cave ale for the first time and felt the fire.
items.reflection: A good ale and a warm forge — what more could one want?

items.memory: When I was 8 years old, listened to the songs of the deep in the great hall.
items.reflection: The stone remembers what the surface forgets.

items.memory: When I was 9 years old, learned to mine in the deep tunnels under the mountain. That year, the world made sense. Nazbal was there too.
items.reflection: Every strike of the pick is a prayer to the deep.

items.memory: In Year 427 (The Sapphire Era, year 56), the world felt heavy with change. What marked that year was Mardgund was born. The defining event was Torgrimbram was born. To this day, I remember it vividly.
items.reflection: The mountain gives, and the mountain takes.

items.memory: The year 428 was a bright one. What struck me most was a great battle was fought — Windoreford against Mokthragorn. What marked that year was Gzolor was born. To this day, I wonder what could have been.
items.reflection: The deep halls are silent today, and that silence speaks.

items.memory: In Year 429 (The Sapphire Era, year 58), the world felt still and waiting. I recorded every transaction in the great ledger for all to see To this day, I wish I could forget. Mokthul was there too.
items.reflection: Gold is heavy, but the mountain is heavier on the soul.

items.memory: I remember Year 430 (The Sapphire Era, year 59) well — it was a age of shadows. What struck me most was Thkragor was born. That year taught me that kindness matters.
items.reflection: I can hear the stone breathing around me.

items.memory: During Year 431 (The Sapphire Era, year 60), I built my legacy. I built a fortress wall that never fell, using the old techniques It was a year that left its mark. That year, the world made sense.
items.reflection: Gold is heavy, but the mountain is heavier on the soul.

items.memory: I recall that in Year 432 (The Sapphire Era, year 61), I witnessed a miracle. I enforced the ancient codes of the mountain without exception To this day, I wish I could forget. All was as it must be, orderly and right.
items.reflection: The mountain gives, and the mountain takes.

items.reflection: The stone remembers what the surface forgets.

items.reflection: The deep halls are silent today, and that silence speaks.

items.memory: A trial was held this season — it was somber.
This spring, I felt the heat settle in in Mokthragorn.
The moon this season looked familiar — I watched it for hours.
My rival Borgornbaz was seen plotting.
I hunted this season and found peace in the work.
A refugee came through Mokthragorn this season and told of war in the east.
This season, I found a lost heirloom.
The snow this season was late — it made travel difficult.
I wonder what the old gods will bring.
This spring, I find myself thinking about the passage of time.
The spring air makes me feel restless.
I have been praying more than usual this season — or perhaps it means nothing at all.
My mother told me that the spring is for war.
I remember this season last year — it was different then.
This season feels charged with meaning — I fear it.
I need to return a favor.
I must prepare for the coming journey.
I will attend the ritual next season.
I want to befriend a neighboring tribe.
I aim to repair the old bridge.
I intend to visit the old shrine.
I am concerned about my reputation — a lie is spreading about me.
I fear that change is coming — and I am not ready for it.
I worry about the old spirits growing restless — the rituals have felt wrong.
I fear that the spirits are angry — they have not answered my prayers.
